name: Currant Bread (Welsh)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream the yeast and sugar, add to half of the warm milk and leave in a warm place for 10-15 minutes until frothy.
2. Rub the lard into the flour and add the dry ingredients (salt, brown sugar, pudding spice).
3. Mix well and make a hole in the middle of the flour mixture.
4. Add the yeast and enough milk to make a soft elastic dough.
5. Cover with a clean cloth and leave in a warm place for 12 hours to rise.
6. Place the dough in a greased, floured tin.
7. Bake in a hot oven (425°F or Mark 7) for 20 minutes.
8. Lower the temperature to 400°F (Mark 6) and bake for a further 45 minutes to 1 hour.
